message:
fix(stdlib): route rand/srand through System V random state for glibc TYPE_3 parity

Per beads bd-rand3, the public `rand()`/`srand()` ABI was diverging
silently from glibc's deterministic streams. glibc uses a TYPE_3
(degree-31 additive feedback shift register) generator and shares
its state machine between `rand`/`srand` and the System V
`random`/`srandom` family. The previous implementation in
`crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdlib/random.rs` used a single
`AtomicU32` plus a one-step linear-congruential step, so for any
seed the first three outputs disagreed with host glibc — e.g. for
`srand(1)` glibc returns `[1804289383, 846930886, 1681692777]`
while the LCG returned `[551763795, 1262442611, 331412042]`.

This commit unifies the two surfaces:

- `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdlib/random.rs`: drop the
  per-`rand` `SEED: AtomicU32` and the `lcg_next` helper. `rand()`
  now delegates to `random_sv::random()` (truncated to `i32`) and
  `srand()` to `random_sv::srandom()`, so the two APIs share state
  exactly the way glibc does. `rand_r` is intentionally untouched
  — it is the POSIX reentrant three-step LCG, a separate algorithm
  with no shared state, and the existing port is already
  bit-exact. The module docstring is rewritten to reflect that
  `rand`/`srand` are now thin wrappers and that `rand_r` remains
  the standalone reentrant variant.
- `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdlib/random_sv.rs`: collapse
  `srandom(0)` to `srandom(1)` inside `RandomState::seed`,
  matching glibc's documented quirk where seed `0` is silently
  rewritten to `1` (otherwise the additive register would never
  leave the all-zero fixed point). Expose a test-only
  `test_global_random_lock()` helper that returns a poison-tolerant
  `MutexGuard<'static, ()>` shared across the two random modules
  so concurrent `cargo test` cannot interleave global state.
- `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdlib/random.rs` tests: take the
  shared lock in `test_srand_then_rand_deterministic`,
  `test_rand_in_range`, and `test_srand_default_seed`, and add a
  `rand_matches_glibc_type3_reference_outputs` table-driven test
  pinning the first six outputs for seeds `[0, 1, 42, ...]`
  against the values dumped from a host-built C program.
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/conformance_diff_stdlib_random.rs`:
  re-enable the `rand`/`srand` arm of the differential harness.
  Declare host `rand` and `srand` as `extern "C"`, add a
  module-local `global_rng_lock()` (independent of the core
  crate's lock because this test process links host glibc as
  well), and step through `RAND_SEEDS = [0, 1, 42, 100, 12345,
  0xDEADBEEF, 0xFFFFFFFF]` running `CALLS_PER_SEED` rounds of
  paired `fl::srand`/`srand` and accumulating divergences in the
  existing `Vec<Divergence>` reporter. The module header is
  rewritten to drop the obsolete "`rand`/`srand` are intentionally
  NOT diff-tested" paragraph.
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/stdlib_abi_test.rs`: pull `rand`
  and `srand` into the `frankenlibc_abi::stdlib_abi` import block
  and add three behavioral checks under the existing
  `random_lock()`: `rand_seeded_deterministic` (same seed →
  identical first call), `rand_shares_random_global_state`
  (`srand(42); rand()` equals `srandom(42); random() as c_int`,
  proving the shared-state contract), and `srand_zero_matches_srand_one`
  (the glibc seed-zero quirk).

The change is internal and ABI-compatible: callers see the same
function signatures and return-value range, just with bit-exact
glibc TYPE_3 outputs and shared global state. No public API
moves; bd-rand3 transitions from open → in_progress in a
follow-up beads sync commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
